DOC_A.full: Cornell Controlled Environment Agriculture — Hydroponic Lettuce Handbook

DOC_B.full: Electrical Conductivity and pH Guide for Hydroponics, HLA-6722

DOC_C.full: Guidelines for Cultivation in a Vertical Farm, Report WPR-1315

DOC_D.full: Photoperiod, Light Intensity and Daily Light Integral — Produce Grower, March 2022

photoperiod_allowed_set.rationale: Derived from MSU/Univ. of Delaware experimental matrix (DOC_D, Table 1). Use these three values for DLI / energy optimisation calculations. Avoid assuming arbitrary intermediate values.

continuous_light_warning.note: Continuous 24 h lighting may cause necrosis, chlorosis and reduced photosynthesis in sensitive cultivars. Industry practice often favours light/dark cycles (e.g. 20/4 h). Lettuce is generally tolerant but cultivar screening is recommended before deploying 24 h.

rockwool_soak_ph.note: One-time soak before seeding; NOT the ongoing nutrient-solution pH.

ph_setpoint.note: No nutrient solution added during first 24 h; seeds irrigated with plain RO water only.

source.context: Configuration and Intensity — first 24 h in germination area

photoperiod_hours.note: 24 h specified for first 24 h to ensure germination. Cultivar risk of continuous-light intolerance is low at this stage.

dli_target.note: Low DLI is intentional — minimises medium drying and prevents seedling stretch.

spectrum_note.value: Blue-rich (fluorescent CWF or broad-spectrum LED); avoid incandescent / red-heavy sources.

temp_night_C.note: No night set-point explicitly given for Day 0; value interpolated from Cornell daytime set-point and general CEA practice.

source.context: Set-points table (day/night 24/19 °C for pond stage used as bounding reference)

humidity_pct.note: Humidity covers kept on to prevent desiccation; target near saturation.

source.context: Day 0 – humidity covers required for bare seed

irrigation.method: Sub-irrigation (Ebb & Flood) or overhead with breaker wand
irrigation.frequency: Every 12 h, 15 min flood, RO water only

rockwool_soak_ph.note: No repeated pre-treatment needed after Day 0 soak.

source.context: Day 1 – fertiliser solution added; pH adjusted to 5.8

ec_increment_above_source_water.note: Cornell specifies 1200 µS/cm (= 1.2 dS/m) ABOVE source-water EC, not absolute EC. Must subtract source-water baseline when reading sensor.

ec_absolute_reference.note: General reference range for lettuce in hydroponics from OSU table (Table 2). Use as sanity-check against increment value.

source.context: Table 2 — Optimum EC range for Lettuce
Days 1–11 — light intensity maintained at 250 µmol/m²/s

photoperiod_hours.note: Continuous light specified for seedling stage at Cornell. Monitor for stretch; blue-rich spectrum counteracts elongation risk.

source.context: Photoperiod 24 h maintained for remaining 10 days in germination area

dli_target.acceptable_range_note: Cornell states ~22 mol/m²/d; anecdotal tolerance up to 30 mol/m²/d for some cultivars.

spectrum_note.value: Blue-rich spectrum; fluorescent CWF or broad-spectrum LED preferred. Incandescent lamps cause etiolation.

temp_day_C.note: Temperature raised to 25 °C from Day 1 onwards in germination area.

temp_night_C.note: Inferred from Cornell pond-stage night set-point (19 °C) as lower bound; 24 h photoperiod means no true night period at this stage.

humidity_pct.note: Covers removed on Day 2; target drops to normal operating range. High humidity encourages botrytis.

irrigation.method: Sub-irrigation (Ebb & Flood) or overhead breaker wand
irrigation.frequency_days_6_to_11: Every 6 h, 15 min flood (increased demand as canopy grows)

vegetative._stage_days: Day 11 – Day 28 (transplant to pond → pre-harvest phase)

substrate_pretreatment.note: Not applicable for pond / NFT / DFT systems.

ph_setpoint.note: pH 5.8 considered optimum for described lettuce system (Cornell). WUR gives acceptable range 4.5–6.5 but notes prolonged excursion causes crop damage and dripper clogging.

source_primary.context: pH section — pH 5.8 optimum, 5.6–6.0 acceptable range

source_secondary.context: §5.5 Nutrient solution — acceptable pH 4.5–6.5; target ~5.5 for most crops

ec_increment_above_source_water.note: Maintained at same increment as seedling stage. Cornell: 1150–1250 µS/cm above source.

source.context: EC set-point: 1150–1250 µS/cm above source water

ec_absolute.note: OSU table gives 1.2–1.8 dS/m absolute for lettuce. WUR notes EC ~1.0–2.5 dS/m normal range depending on crop and growth stage.

ppfd_options.note: Low intensity, moderate photoperiod — energy-efficient option.
Balanced baseline treatment used in MSU experiments.
Good yield/energy ratio. At DLI 15.6 mol/m²/d, lower PPFD × longer photoperiod outperformed higher PPFD × shorter.
Highest yield treatment in MSU study; continuous light risk must be screened per cultivar.

recommended_default.rationale: Best yield-per-energy compromise from MSU dataset; avoids continuous-light physiology risk flagged by WUR.

dli_range.upper_reference: Cornell operational target 17 mol/m²/d with supplemental HPS; MSU max tested 15.6 mol/m²/d with LEDs.

source_primary.context: Table 1 — DLI treatments from 6.9 to 15.6 mol/m²/d

tipburn_airflow_dependency.note: Cornell could not exceed 12 mol/m²/d without vertical downward airflow before tipburn occurred. Paddle fans blowing 140 CFM/ft² of pond area are required when targeting DLI > 12 mol/m²/d.

spectrum_recommendation.value: 50% warm-white + 50% red (peak 664 nm) for production; broad spectrum (blue 5–10%, green ~10%, red ~80%) as safe baseline.

temp_day_C.note: Maximum dry weight production at 24 °C air / 24 °C water (Thompson et al. cited in Cornell). Growth rate declines above 31 °C; cool water can partially compensate high air temperature.

source.context: Water Temperature — no higher than 25 °C, cool at 26 °C, heat at 24 °C
Set-points — RH minimum 50%, no higher than 70%

co2_ppm.note: CO2 enrichment to 1000–1500 ppm accelerates growth; supply from liquid CO2 tank (not heaters — contaminants slow lettuce growth).

dissolved_oxygen_ppm.note: Crop failure risk below 3 ppm. Supply pure O2 to pond; air-stone aeration insufficient for high-density production.

source.context: Set-points — DO 7 ppm; crop failure < 3 ppm

airflow.note: Paddle fan mandatory above DLI 12 mol/m²/d to drive transpiration and prevent tipburn via calcium transport.

ph_setpoint.note: Maintain same setpoint as vegetative stage. Do not initiate new long-duration chemical corrections within 7 days of harvest.

source.context: EC 1150–1250 µS/cm above source water (general operational)

ec_absolute.note: Slight EC elevation towards upper bound can marginally improve leaf quality but reduces yield. Do not raise EC > 2.0 dS/m near harvest.

solution_refresh.recommendation: Full nutrient solution replacement every 14 days to prevent accumulation of sodium and other non-absorbed ions.

source.context: Refreshing the Nutrient Solution — replace completely every two weeks

photoperiod_hours.note: Reduced to 16 h as energy-saving measure; DLI still adequate for final head fill. Avoids new stress induction days before harvest.

source.context: Table 1 — 16 h photoperiod treatments tested (MSU); WUR industry note: electricity cost discourages extended photoperiod near end of cycle

temp_day_C.note: Lower end of range accepted to slow respiration and extend post-harvest shelf life.

source.context: Air Temperature set-point 24 °C Day — maintained; slight reduction for quality is common practice

co2_ppm.note: Maintain enrichment until lights-out on harvest day to maximise final biomass accumulation.

post_harvest.float_sanitation: Clean Styrofoam floats with 2% bleach solution between crop cycles.

source.context: Day 35 harvest; post-harvest storage 40 °F; float cleaning
